# Technologies Section — Modern Landing Page Redesign

## User Story

**As a** prospective client evaluating the Interview Coach service,  
**I want to** see a grouped overview of the technologies and tools used,  
**so that** I can quickly verify that the practice works with the stack relevant to my needs.

---

## Description

This story delivers the Technologies section: a badge rail where technology tags are grouped into capability bands (e.g., Cloud, AI/ML, Frontend, Backend). On pointer devices, badges have a subtle hover effect. The section conveys breadth of expertise at a glance without requiring detailed reading.

**Business value:** Displaying the technology stack acts as a passive trust signal for technical buyers evaluating fit.

---

## Scenario

### Scenario 1: Visitor scrolls to the technologies section

**Given** a visitor scrolls to the technologies section  
**When** the section enters the viewport  
**Then** a section heading is visible  
**And** technology badges are displayed, grouped visually by capability band  
**And** each capability band has a label identifying its category (e.g., "Cloud", "AI & ML")

### Scenario 2: Visitor hovers over a badge (pointer device)

**Given** the visitor is on a device with a hover-capable pointer  
**When** they move the cursor over a technology badge  
**Then** the badge displays a visible hover effect (e.g., background tint, border accent, or slight lift)  
**And** the transition uses the shared motion timing token

### Scenario 3: Visitor views the section on a touch device

**Given** the visitor is on a mobile or tablet device  
**When** the technologies section is visible  
**Then** all badges are visible in their default (non-hovered) state  
**And** badges wrap naturally without horizontal overflow  
**And** capability group labels remain readable

### Scenario 4: Visitor views technologies on mobile

**Given** the visitor is on a viewport narrower than 768px  
**When** the technologies section loads  
**Then** capability bands stack vertically  
**And** badges within each band wrap to multiple lines as needed  
**And** no horizontal scrolling is required

---

## Acceptance Criteria

### Core Functionality
- [ ] Technologies are displayed as labelled badge/pill elements
- [ ] Badges are visually grouped by capability band (minimum 3 distinct bands)
- [ ] Each capability band has a visible category label
- [ ] Section includes a heading identifying it as the technologies section

### Interaction
- [ ] Hover effect applies on pointer devices only (use `@media (hover: hover)`)
- [ ] Hover effect is visible and consistent with the design token accent colour
- [ ] Hover transition uses the shared motion timing CSS token
- [ ] No hover styles are visible in the default state on touch devices

### Responsive Layout
- [ ] On desktop: capability bands are arranged in a horizontal or multi-column rail
- [ ] On mobile (<768px): bands stack vertically; badges wrap without overflow
- [ ] No horizontal scrollbar appears at any standard viewport width

### Accessibility
- [ ] Technology labels are readable text (not purely icon-based)
- [ ] Badge elements do not carry interactive semantics unless they link to something
- [ ] Section heading is at the correct semantic level
- [ ] Sufficient colour contrast on badge text against badge background

---

## Prerequisites

- [Hero Section — Modern Landing Page Redesign](./hero-section-landing-page.md) — design tokens must be in place before badge styles are applied
